verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family member that is predominantly cytosolic in healthy cells. Upon apoptotic stimulus, it translocates to and inserts into the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM) via a C-terminal transmembrane domain, where it oligomerizes to form pores and release cytochrome c. The protein has no documented plasma membrane surface accessibility—it is exclusively an intracellular protein functioning at mitochondrial membranes, not at the cell surface.
